title: dryvist homelab ansible_inventory contract
description: schema_version 2.0.0. Shape of the ansible_inventory.json artifact written by dryvist/terraform-proxmox (inventory_publish.tf, local.ansible_inventory) to the S3 state backend and consumed by dryvist/ansible-proxmox, dryvist/ansible-proxmox-apps, and dryvist/ansible-splunk through the shared inventory_resolve role. v2 reconciles the schema to the REAL producer output (issue #134): the top-level keys are exactly what inventory_publish.tf emits. Breaking changes bump the major and add a versions/v<X.Y.Z>/ snapshot.

schema_version.description: Semantic version of this contract. Consumers fail-fast when the major differs. Emitted by terraform-proxmox inventory_publish.tf; must be '2.x.y' for this schema.

domain.description: Internal DNS domain for FQDN resolution (e.g. 'example.com'). MAY be empty: terraform-proxmox supports an empty domain (locals.tf falls back to a bare hostname). The ansible-proxmox-apps loader separately requires a non-empty domain at runtime, since per-node ansible_host is derived as {node-role}.{domain}.

containers.description: LXC containers managed by terraform-proxmox (community.proxmox.proxmox_pct_remote connection). Map of logical key -> container metadata. May be empty ({}).

vms.description: VMs (SSH connection) managed by terraform-proxmox. Map of logical key -> VM metadata. May be empty ({}).

docker_vms.description: Subset of vms tagged 'docker' (e.g. the CI runners host). Map of logical key -> VM metadata. May be empty ({}).

splunk_vm.description: Splunk Enterprise VM (vendor-only Docker deployment). Single-key object keyed 'splunk'.

constants.description: Pipeline constants from terraform-proxmox local.pipeline_constants: the single source of truth for service / syslog / netflow / notification / vector-DB and other port families plus their routing maps, surfaced to consumers via the artifact. New port families are additive: additionalProperties accepts any further map (portMap or routing map), so adding one upstream does NOT force a schema major bump or break pinned consumers. The five families consumers depend on are required and strictly typed as portMap.

ingress.description: Traefik HTTPS ingress route table (local.ingress): one route per fronted service UI. Consumed by the ansible-proxmox-apps traefik + technitium_dns roles. May be empty ([]).

ingress_vip.description: keepalived VRRP virtual IP that fronted-service DNS records point at. Empty string when fewer than two ingress instances exist (keepalived never binds the VIP), so the technitium_dns role falls back to the single Traefik host.

ingress_hosts.description: Advertised address of each ingress instance (the keepalived unicast_peer members): a static host IP or a DNS-first FQDN. May be empty ([]).

host_services.description: Host-level service config (e.g. NAS via ZFS + Samba) consumed by ansible-proxmox. Free-form per-service object so Ansible roles own validation. May be empty ({}).

nodes.description: Proxmox cluster node inventory (non-secret identity). ansible-proxmox targets these and skips nodes where commissioned = false. May be empty ({}).

node_storage.description: Per-node ZFS pools/datasets/quotas for ansible-proxmox to provision; Terraform only references the datastore by id. May be empty ({}).

ipv4OrHostname.description: Static guests: IPv4 address. DHCP-first guests: hostname/FQDN (a DNS A record resolves it to the DHCP reservation).

routingMap.description: name -> routing object (e.g. syslog_port_map, ai_log_routing). Inner objects carry Splunk index/sourcetype plus port fields; extra fields are tolerated so upstream can extend routing metadata without a schema major bump.

mac.description: Deterministic locally-administered MAC for DHCP-first guests (02: prefix); null for static guests.

reserved_ip.description: DHCP-reservation address for DHCP-first guests (UniFi pins the MAC here; the DNS A record resolves here); null for static guests.

mac.description: Deterministic locally-administered MAC for DHCP-first VMs (02: prefix); null/absent for static VMs.

reserved_ip.description: DHCP-reservation address for DHCP-first VMs; null/absent for static VMs.

ingressRoute.description: One fronted service. Mirrors the inventory_publish.tf precondition: name + port > 0 + (a single-backend ip OR a load-balanced backends pool).

backends.description: Load-balanced backend pool (apex or named route). Members are static host IPs or DNS-first FQDNs.

apex.description: Host rule is the base domain itself (no <name>. prefix).

role.description: Role label used to derive the per-node management FQDN {role}.{domain}.

hardware.description: Capability-neutral hardware class label; null when unset.

commissioned.description: false = declared but not yet installed; ansible-proxmox skips it.
